course_description:
All psychological phenomena are based in the brain and nervous system. This course overviews the rapidly expanding discipline of neuroscience as relevant to psychologists. Topics include neural changes during learning, cortical control of executive functions and language, control of movement and skilled actions as well as the biological bases of common mental disorders.

The main aim of the course is to develop a critical understanding of neuroscience so that knowledge relevant to psychology in this expanding field can be evaluated by the student. Our approach is a bottom-up hierarchical one: sensory and perceptual processes are essential knowledge for the understanding of neuroscience principles. A secondary aim is to inculcate a feeling of interest in some students who may want to go on to study more advanced topics such as human neuropsychology, sensory neuroscience, or the neuroscience of social behaviour, and prepare them with the methodological background to approach these issues.ﾠTo this end, we will be spending some tutorial time learning a few basic principles of programming in Matlab, as preparation for 3rd-year, honours and potentially PhD-level pursuits.ﾠKnowledge of methodology is fundamental to conceptualising behavioural and neuroscience principles, therefore methodology will be interwoven into all topics covered in the tutorials.ﾠ

course_aims: The main aim of the course is to develop a critical understanding of neuroscience so that knowledge relevant to psychology in this expanding field can be evaluated by the student. Our approach is a bottom-up hierarchical one: sensory and perceptual processes are essential knowledge for the understanding of neuroscience principles. A secondary aim is to inculcate a feeling of interest in some students who may want to go on to study more advanced topics such as human neuropsychology, sensory neuroscience, or the neuroscience of social behaviour, and prepare them with the methodological background to approach these issues.ﾠTo this end, we will be spending some tutorial time learning a few basic principles of programming in Matlab, as preparation for 3rd-year, honours and potentially PhD-level pursuits.ﾠKnowledge of methodology is fundamental to conceptualising behavioural and neuroscience principles, therefore methodology will be interwoven into all topics covered in the tutorials.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: locate basic brain structures and use anatomical terms
identify types of brain cells and their functions
specify how signals are transmitted through neurons
specify the consequences of damage to certain brain regions
define some key neurological syndromes (for example, agnosia, apraxia)
specify the distinctions between several types of key neurological syndromes
define and explain basic facts about how neurons, neurotransmitters and complex brain systems operate to produce normal and abnormal cognition, emotions, motivation and behaviour
demonstrate understanding of facts in tests and written reports
write an empirical report about a behavioural experiment, clearly defining theory, methods, data and results and inferences to be drawn from data and results
demonstrate, through active laboratory activities, a knowledge of some key perceptual functions.
demonstrate, through active laboratory activities, an understanding of selected cognitive functions.
understand and explain how both low-level abilities, such as perception and motor control, and higher-level abilities, such as inhibitory control and working memory, are organised in the brain
Identify and explain the range of research methods used in neuroscience
Understand some preliminary principles of computer programming (Matlab)

assessment_details.task_description: Eight (of the 11) tutorials are content tutorials. A short answer worksheet (typically 2 or 3 questions each requiring 50-100 words) based on the tutorial activity is to be completed in the final 20 minutes of the tutorial and handed in at the end of the tutorial. Each worksheet will be worth 5 marks and the best 7 worksheets will form the total worksheet mark (out of 35). There are no options for deferral or late submission. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
There are no extensions or deferrals available. To account for this, only the best 7 of 8 submissions count towards the final mark.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
